topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notification blinked on my phone, a dull, irritating reminder of the previous occupant's tenancy ending. A package. It was addressed to me, but the return address clearly indicated the unit I'd vacated just last month. My jaw tightened as I looked at the plain brown box sitting on my doorstep. I snatched it up, the cardboard feeling cheap and insulting in my hands. Inside, nestled in packing peanuts, was a collection of novelty mugs—the kind with ridiculous cartoon characters. I stared at the assortment, a slow, cold burn starting low in my stomach. I didn't even bother to check the return label. I marched straight to the recycling bin, dropping the entire thing in with unnecessary force, making sure the sound echoed in the quiet hallway.

[story 2]

I watched the courier leave, my shoulders stiffening as I surveyed the unsolicited delivery. It was clearly meant for the person who’d lived here before me, a ghost from a previous lease. A tight knot formed just beneath my ribs, a sensation like a cold stone settling in my chest. I picked up the parcel, my fingers brushing against the rough paper. I didn't need to open it to know what it was: another piece of clutter from someone who didn't appreciate the space they occupied. With a deliberate, exaggerated sigh, I carried it not to the bin, but across the room, placing it squarely on top of the bookshelf, obscuring a favorite, well-loved novel. I smoothed down my shirt, a small, sharp smirk playing on my lips before I turned away, determined to make the presence of the item undeniable.

[story 3]

When the doorbell rang, I hesitated for only a fraction of a second before opening the door. There it was, the package, clearly addressed to me, yet bearing the faded details of the previous tenant’s address. A surge of irritation flared hot behind my eyes. I took the box, my grip surprisingly firm, and carried it back into my apartment without a word. I didn't examine the contents; I simply tossed the box onto the kitchen counter with a loud thud that made the ceramic mugs rattle. I stood there, arms crossed, watching the box sit there, a silent monument to the inconvenience of the past, feeling a deep, simmering resentment that settled heavy and unwelcome in my chest.
